untranslatable_reason: The signal requires ranking constituents by a custom 12-month momentum metric (log(price_t-1 / price_t-12)) and selecting top-ranked stocks. This cross-sectional ranking and relative selection logic cannot be expressed using point-in-time indicator comparisons. The backtest engine would need portfolio construction and ranking capabilities beyond single-instrument indicator conditions. The signal also requires: (1) measuring momentum over a 12-month period excluding the last month, (2) ranking NIFTY100 constituents against each other, and (3) selecting a portfolio based on that ranking—none of which are expressible with the allowed operators and indicators.
translation_notes: This is a factor-based, cross-sectional momentum strategy that requires portfolio-level ranking logic. The allowed indicator set is designed for single-instrument technical analysis (RSI, moving averages, etc.) and cannot express: (a) custom lookback calculations like log(price_t-1 / price_t-12), (b) relative ranking across a universe of stocks, or (c) portfolio selection by rank. A proper implementation would require access to a ranking/sorting function at the portfolio construction level, not condition-based entry/exit rules.
